name: Continents & Countries
description: World geography — continents, countries, and capitals

questions.q: How many continents are there on Earth by the traditional count?

source.notes: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia/Oceania, Europe, North America, South America.

questions.q: Which is the largest country in the world by land area?

source.notes: About 17.1 million km²; spans 11 time zones.

questions.q: Which is the largest continent by land area?

source.notes: Covers about 30% of Earth's land.

questions.q: Egypt is located on which continent?

source.notes: The Sinai Peninsula technically lies in Asia.

questions.q: What is the official language of Brazil?

source.notes: Brazil was colonized by Portugal from 1500.

questions.q: Australia/Oceania is the smallest continent by what measure?

source.notes: Only Antarctica has less population.

questions.q: The Statue of Liberty in New York was a gift to the United States from which country?

source.notes: Dedicated October 28, 1886 for the U.S. centennial.

questions.q: The Swiss Franc is the official currency of which country?

source.notes: Liechtenstein also uses the Swiss Franc.

questions.q: The Baht is the official currency of which Southeast Asian country?

source.notes: Divided into 100 satang.

questions.q: The Zloty is the currency of which European country?

source.notes: Means 'golden' in Polish.

questions.q: What is the currency of Brazil?

source.notes: Introduced in 1994 to replace the Cruzeiro Real.

questions.q: What is the capital of Serbia?

source.notes: Situated at the confluence of the Sava and Danube rivers.

questions.q: Thailand was previously known by what name until 1939?

source.notes: Briefly reverted to Siam 1946–1948, then permanently Thailand.

questions.q: In 2018, the African country Swaziland officially renamed itself to what?

source.notes: Means 'land of the Swazis'; change announced by King Mswati III on the 50th anniversary of independence.

questions.q: Which country lies on the western shore of the Caspian Sea?

source.notes: Baku, the Azerbaijani capital, is on the Caspian coast.

questions.q: Which is the largest country in the Americas by land area?

source.notes: Canada is the second-largest country in the world after Russia.

questions.q: Which country holds the southernmost point of the South American mainland?

source.notes: Patagonia's Cape Horn region.

questions.q: The Caucasus Mountains are traditionally seen as the border between which two continents?

source.notes: Along with the Ural Mountains to the north.

questions.q: In what year was the first Eurovision Song Contest held?

source.notes: Held in Lugano, Switzerland; only seven countries competed.

questions.q: What was the Roman name for the island that is now Ireland?

source.notes: Related to the Greek 'Iernē'.

questions.q: What is the name of the supercontinent that existed about 200 million years ago before breaking apart?

source.notes: Broke up into Laurasia and Gondwana.

questions.q: Which country proudly shares its name with the chemical element of atomic number 84?

source.notes: Polonium was discovered by Marie and Pierre Curie in 1898, named for Marie's homeland.
